summary: Iter25 backend regression for cyclomatic-complexity refactor — 100% green. Full pytest suite: 449 passed, 2 skipped (identical to pre-refactor baseline). New focused refactor regression file (test_refactor_regression_iter25.py, 16 tests) exercises every branch of the refactored helpers in update_withdrawal, admin_revenue, generate_audit_pdf, generate_vip_closing_pdf end-to-end via curl-style HTTP. All pass.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/admin.py — file is now 1235 lines. Above the recommended 700-line ceiling. Consider splitting per concern: withdrawals.py, revenue.py, company_funds.py, audit_export.py, users.py. Pure organizational, no functional risk, but will pay off as more endpoints land.

_validate_paid_evidence relies on the implicit invariant that update_doc is mutated by _collect_payout_evidence BEFORE validation. The current call order in update_withdrawal is correct (collect → validate), but a future contributor could swap these and silently break the 400 branch. Adding a one-line comment near the call site, or making _validate_paid_evidence take both proof+hash as explicit args, would harden against this.

_refund_balance_on_reject uses amount_usd as the increment regardless of the currency it is keyed under (vip_balances.{currency}). For non-USD withdrawals this credits the wrong magnitude (e.g. a 25 USD-equivalent BRL withdrawal refunds 25 to vip_balances.BRL, not 25 * USDBRL). This is pre-existing behavior preserved by the refactor — flagging here because it's now isolated in a single helper and easy to fix later.

admin_revenue still calls compute_order_profit inside a python loop (await per order). For ≥5000 approved orders this is N awaits. Acceptable today but a candidate for batch precomputation / aggregation later.
audit_pdf.py and pdf_service.py refactors look clean — helpers are small, naming is consistent, no shared mutable state between helpers.
test_credentials: Used autouse session seeding from conftest._autoseed_sessions: test_session_admin_X (user_test_admin01), test_session_employee_X, test_session_vip_X, test_session_normal_X. TOTP secret JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXPJBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP. No real auth flow exercised.
seed_data_creation: Per-test withdrawal rows prefixed TEST_wd_* inserted directly into the withdrawals collection (and cleaned up in finally blocks) to deterministically drive every branch of update_withdrawal. vip_balances.USD increment from the reject-refund test is rolled back in finally to keep the suite idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The refactor was purely structural and is fully covered now: branches (a)-(h) of update_withdrawal, full JSON shape of /admin/revenue (with and without ?days=30), %PDF magic-byte check on both /vip/daily-closing and /admin/audit/export.pdf (with and without filters), and 7-section health summary. If the next iteration touches any of these files, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression_iter25.py first — it is fast (~4s) and isolates this refactor's contract. Pre-existing behavior note for next agent: _refund_balance_on_reject refunds amount_usd into vip_balances.{currency} verbatim — this is unit-mismatched for non-USD but PRE-EXISTING (refactor preserved it intentionally). Don't 'fix' it without checking the seed/order flow.
rca of the issue: No issue. Refactor is behavior-identical: same signatures, same HTTP codes, same JSON shapes, same error messages, same PDF magic bytes.
